title: Heart Rate Threshold Rules
description: A walkthrough of the Heart Rate source and its supervisor-style Threshold Rules behaviors.

steps.title: Open Vision -> Heart Rate
steps.body: This tutorial covers the Heart Rate source. If you do not see the Heart Rate tab, include it in the Vision source selector on the Core page first.

steps.title: Source Tab
steps.body: The Heart Rate source contributes BPM as hidden sensory input. It is the same source-and-child-addon model as the visual sources, but the signal is physiological rather than visual.

steps.title: Threshold Rules Tab
steps.body: Now switch to Threshold Rules. This addon uses the same persona pattern as the Supervisor family, but its first field is called Signal Trigger because the source data is BPM, not an image.

steps.title: Signal Trigger and Action
steps.body: Each behavior pairs a heart-rate state with a response style. For example: above 120 BPM, below 50 BPM, sustained elevation for several cycles, or recovery after an earlier spike.

steps.title: Same Behavior Language
steps.body: Even though the source is different, the underlying authoring language is now the same: persona, trigger, action, strictness, and emotion override. That consistency is what makes it easy to learn.

steps.title: Best Use
steps.body: Heart Rate Threshold Rules are a strong fit for coaching, concern, teasing, workout callouts, and any persona-driven reaction that should depend on a measurable BPM state rather than guesswork.
